comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Both blocks self-inflicted by my scope edit (setup-project.sh) and now remediated with an AC+proving command (grep -F Autosubmit infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh) + commit-message requirement. Advisory items are operator-runbook granularity (curl/auth/jq/rollback for the cutover, which is operator-attested) or impact-0.0/YAGNI. Full triage in session log 547b.".

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The auto-lander needs an opt-in, machine-readable "please land this" intent that agents set and the bot consumes as a lease. Under Fast-Forward-Only `main` (ADR-0040) the label must be **non-gating** (FFO already enforces up-to-date, so gating would be redundant and add a wedge) and **non-sticky** (it is about the current tip). Research (GerriScary/CVE-2025-1568) requires a tight vote ACL.

> **Name collision:** `Autosubmit` must be a **project-authored label**, NOT Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` submit-plugin (lowercase) — the plugin auto-submits on gate satisfaction, which is not what we want. Author our own label.

## What
Author an `Autosubmit` Gerrit label in `infra/gerrit/project.config` and apply it to live `refs/meta/config` (operator cutover — detect-only under ADR-0026). Vote ACL granted to **`Contributors` (requesters) AND `Administrators`** (matching the existing `LLM-Review`/`Verified` two-line pattern, so admins retain a manual set/clear fallback). Canonical ACL lines: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors` **and** `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators`. `function = NoBlock`; empty `copyCondition`. Setting the label only REQUESTS a land (non-gating); the bot still requires `is:submittable` (both real votes MAX) and re-verifies at submit, so a requester-votable ACL is not a security vector. **This canonical account/group naming is what the epic and downstream stories reference.**

## Scope
`project.config` label block + vote ACL + the pre-cutover access snapshot + the live `refs/meta/config` cutover, PLUS a `setup-project.sh` commit-message/update touch so the cutover is reflected in the audit trail. The `Autosubmit` label block's **header comment in `project.config` is S1's own security-invariant artifact** (see AC below): it records, inline where the label is defined, why the label cannot weaken the two-vote gate. The full ADR-0042 **is authored downstream by S6 (`broken-checkered-tiger`)** — S1 does NOT create or amend ADR-0042; S1 supplies the config facts + snapshot that ADR-0042's security section later consolidates and diffs against (forward reference). The service that reads the label is a separate story (S2).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Autosubmit` label added to `infra/gerrit/project.config` with `-1..+1`, `function = NoBlock`, empty `copyCondition` (drops on every new patchset), and explicit value lines: `value = 0 No score`, `value = +1 Land this change`, and `value = -1 Do not land` (the `-1..+1` range is fixed, so all three value lines are required — the `-1` line is NOT optional).
- [ ] `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL granted via **two** lines in `[access "refs/heads/*"]` — `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors` (requesters set it under their own identity via S4's `land`) **and** `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` (manual operator fallback: set/clear a stale vote), mirroring the existing `LLM-Review`/`Verified` two-line grants. `addPatchSet` cannot forge it (empty `copyCondition` drops it on every new patchset).
- [ ] Label is NOT referenced by any submit requirement (non-gating; FFO enforces up-to-date structurally).
- [ ] **Pre-cutover access baseline captured BEFORE the label is pushed:** save the current `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` response to `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` and commit it in the SAME change that adds the label to `project.config`, so ADR-0042's security-invariants access-diff has a committed baseline. (Capture happens before the `refs/meta/config` push; the label add and the snapshot commit ship together.)
- [ ] `config-check` passes; live cutover applied to `refs/meta/config` and verified via `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`; a post-cutover `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` diffs cleanly against `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` showing ONLY the added `label-Autosubmit` vote grants (no other ACL drift).
- [ ] **Security-invariants artifact (S1's own deliverable — an inline header comment, NOT the downstream ADR):** the `[label "Autosubmit"]` block in `infra/gerrit/project.config` carries a header comment recording the "non-gating label + bot re-verifies the two-vote gate on the exact tested SHA at submit" invariant (the protection is the re-verify at submit, NOT restricting who may set `Autosubmit`) with its concrete checks — a `grep` of `infra/gerrit/project.config` proving `function = NoBlock` and no submit-requirement reference, and the note that the bot only submits when `is:submittable` holds on the tested SHA — demonstrating the label cannot weaken the two-vote (`LLM-Review`+`Verified`) gate. The header comment also notes the **name collision** (this is a project-authored `Autosubmit` label, NOT Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` submit-plugin, which we deliberately do not use). **S6's ADR-0042 later consolidates this invariant (forward reference); S1 does not author ADR-0042.**
- [ ] **`setup-project.sh` reflects the cutover:** `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` is updated so its `refs/meta/config` push commit message names the `Autosubmit` label + ACL addition (audit trail). **Proving command:** `grep -F 'Autosubmit' infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` returns a match.
compiled_state.title: S1 Autosubmit label + ACLs (project.config + refs/meta/config cutover)

verify_commands.command: git config -f infra/gerrit/project.config --get-regexp label.Autosubmit && ! grep -q "submittableIf.*Autosubmit" infra/gerrit/project.config
